description:
You are assisting a localisation engineer who must do two things in one working session:

    1.  Update a mock user-level firewall policy so that the static-site preview service they run on TCP port 8080 can be reached from the local network.
    2.  Add a brand-new French translation file to the project’s translations directory.

    The engineer does NOT have root privileges, so the real system firewall cannot be touched.  
    Instead, the team keeps a text-based “firewall policy” in the user’s home directory that downstream scripts parse to build container rules.  
    Your job is to edit that text file in the correct, auditable way and then create a structured log of the change.  
    Afterwards you will generate the new translation file.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PRE-EXISTING MATERIALS
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1.  Directory that holds the user-level firewall policy:

        /home/user/firewall/

    • /home/user/firewall/active_rules.conf  (already exists)

        Current contents (exact, six lines):
            # Firewall Rules
            # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
            # default policy : deny incoming, allow outgoing
            # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
            # BEGIN RULES
            # END RULES

2.  The web-site localisation project:

        /home/user/localise-site/

    • /home/user/localise-site/templates/messages.pot  (portable object template)

        The first 17 lines of this template file are the header that must be copied
        verbatim into any new *.po file, after which certain header fields are
        customised.  For testing purposes, the header section ends with an empty line.
        (The template’s body with msgid/msgstr pairs continues after the blank line.)

    • There are currently no *.po files inside /home/user/localise-site/.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
WHAT YOU MUST DO
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STEP A — Add a new firewall rule

1. Append ONE new line immediately above the literal marker  “# END RULES”  
   in /home/user/firewall/active_rules.conf.  
2. That line must be exactly (including spacing and comment text):

        ALLOW IN 8080/tcp   # Localisation Preview

   • Use spaces, not tabs.  
   • Do not modify any existing line other than inserting the one new rule.  
   • The rule must be placed on its own line.

STEP B — Write an audit log of the change

1. Create the directory     /home/user/firewall/logs/    if it does not exist.
2. Inside that directory create a log file named  firewall_status.log
3. The log file must contain precisely three lines in the exact order below:

        TIMESTAMP: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC
        ACTION: Added rule for port 8080/tcp
        STATUS: SUCCESS

   • Replace YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS with the current UTC date and time at
     the moment you write the file, using 24-hour clock, zero-padded.
   • There must be a single space after each colon.
   • No extra blank lines are allowed before, between or after the three lines.

STEP C — Create the French translation file

1. Inside  /home/user/localise-site/  create a new file  fr.po
2. Copy the first 17 lines (the header section) from
   /home/user/localise-site/templates/messages.pot  directly into fr.po.
3. Immediately after pasting those 17 lines, update the following header fields
   in fr.po so they read (values must be double-quoted, followed by \n as shown):

        "Language: fr\n"
        "Language-Team: French Team\n"
        "PO-Revision-Date: YYYY-MM-DD 00:00+0000\n"

   • Replace YYYY-MM-DD with today’s UTC date (same date used in the log).
   • Leave all other header fields untouched.
4. After the header’s terminating blank line, leave the rest of fr.po empty;
   the body of the file will be filled in later by translators.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
HOW AUTOMATED TESTS WILL VERIFY YOUR WORK
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. /home/user/firewall/active_rules.conf  
   • File exists.  
   • Exactly one new line reading  
         ALLOW IN 8080/tcp   # Localisation Preview  
     appears immediately before the “# END RULES” marker.

2. /home/user/firewall/logs/firewall_status.log  
   • File exists.  
   • Contains exactly three lines formatted as specified.  
   • TIMESTAMP line’s datetime parses as a valid UTC time not more than
     24 hours in the past.

3. /home/user/localise-site/fr.po  
   • File exists.  
   • First 17 lines identical to the template’s first 17 lines EXCEPT that the
     three header fields mentioned above contain the new values.  
   • The file ends with a single newline right after the header’s blank line
     (i.e., no message entries yet).

Follow the instructions precisely so that automated grading scripts can find the files and match their contents byte-for-byte (except for dates/timestamps where specified).  
Do not print the entire files to the console unless you want to double-check your work—you will be marked only on the filesystem state described above.
truth:
<!-- File that must exist before the exercise starts -->
    <initial_file path="/home/user/firewall/active_rules.conf"><![CDATA[
# Firewall Rules
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# default policy : deny incoming, allow outgoing
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# BEGIN RULES
# END RULES
]]></initial_file>

    <!-- Final expected contents for strict verification -->
    <final_file path="/home/user/firewall/active_rules.conf"><![CDATA[
# Firewall Rules
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# default policy : deny incoming, allow outgoing
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# BEGIN RULES
ALLOW IN 8080/tcp   # Localisation Preview
# END RULES
]]></final_file>

    <!-- Log file format pattern (regex friendly) -->
    <log_file path="/home/user/firewall/logs/firewall_status.log">
        Line 1 must match regex: ^TIMESTAMP: \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2} UTC$
        Line 2 must be exactly:  ACTION: Added rule for port 8080/tcp
        Line 3 must be exactly:  STATUS: SUCCESS
    </log_file>

    <!-- Template header lines that must be copied into fr.po BEFORE editing -->
    <template_header_line_count>17</template_header_line_count>

    <!-- Regex snippets that must appear in the new fr.po after editing -->
    <po_checks>
        Contains line exactly: "Language: fr\n"
        Contains line exactly: "Language-Team: French Team\n"
        Contains line matching: ^"PO-Revision-Date: \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} 00:00\+0000\\n"$
    </po_checks>
